subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Modules
- **1** modular game board (Lord Eradikus's spaceship) — double-sided module tiles that connect together
- **Command module** (starting area)
- **Multiple ship modules** with rooms, corridors, and data ports

#### Cards
- **Starting deck cards** — 10 per player (basic movement, attack, and Stumble cards)
- **Dungeon/Market deck** — large deck of purchasable cards
- **Reserve cards** — always available for purchase (basic upgrades)
- **Boss attack cards** — Lord Eradikus events

#### Tokens & Cubes
- **Clank cubes** — 30+ per player in their color
- **Dragon bag** — black cloth bag for Clank resolution
- **Black Clank cubes** — added to bag, harmless when drawn
- **Artifact tokens** — worth varying VP
- **Secret tokens** — face-down bonus VP scattered on the board
- **Credit tokens** — currency for purchasing cards
- **Health cubes** — track player damage
- **Hack tokens** — required to hack command posts
- **Key tokens** — open locked passages

#### Player Components
- **Player pawns** — 1 per player
- **Backpack boards** — optional, for organizing tokens

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Assemble the ship** — Connect module tiles as shown in the scenario setup. The command module is at the top.
2. **Place artifacts** — Put artifact tokens on designated spaces throughout the ship. Higher-value artifacts are deeper in the ship.
3. **Place secrets** — Put face-down secret tokens on marked spaces.
4. **Market row** — Deal 6 cards from the dungeon deck face-up as the market row.
5. **Reserve cards** — Place always-available reserve piles nearby.
6. **Dragon bag** — Put 24 black cubes in the dragon bag.
7. **Data ports** — Mark the hackable command post locations on the board.
8. **Escape pods** — Located near the command module. You must reach one to escape.
9. **Bounty hunter track** — Lord Eradikus's threat track along the side.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Receives
- **10 starting deck cards** — 6 basic cards, 2 Stumble cards (generate Clank), and 2 other starter cards
- **30 Clank cubes** in their player color
- **1 player pawn** — place in the starting area (command module cargo bay)
- **Hack token** — start with 0

#### Starting Area
- All players begin in the cargo bay area of the command module.
- No artifacts or secrets are accessible from the start — you must venture deeper into the ship.

#### Shuffle & Draw
- Shuffle your 10-card starting deck and draw 5 cards as your opening hand.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **Starting hand** — Draw 5 cards from your shuffled deck.
- **Objective** — Steal an artifact from deep in the ship and escape via an escape pod.
- **No Clank at start** — Your Clank area starts empty.
- **Market available** — 6 market cards plus reserve piles are ready for purchase.
- **Lord Eradikus** — The boss has not attacked yet. Boss attacks occur when specific cards appear in the market row.
- **First player** — Chosen randomly. Play proceeds clockwise.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn:

1. **Play cards** — Play all 5 cards from your hand (or choose not to play some).
2. **Generate resources** — Cards produce **Skill** (currency to buy cards), **Swords** (attack), **Boots** (movement), and sometimes **Clank** (noise).
3. **Add Clank** — For each Clank symbol on played cards, add one of your colored cubes to the Clank area on the board.
4. **Spend resources** — Use Boots to move through the ship, Swords to fight enemies on the board, Skill to buy cards from the market.
5. **Collect tokens** — Pick up artifacts, secrets, credits, or hack tokens from your current room.
6. **Refill market** — Replace purchased cards from the dungeon deck.
7. **Boss attacks** — If a Boss attack card is revealed when refilling the market, resolve an attack.
8. **Discard & Draw** — Discard all played cards and draw 5 new cards.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Movement
- Spend **Boot icons** to move between connected rooms.
- Some passages are **locked** — require a Key token.
- Some passages are **one-way** or require hacking a data port first.
- **Teleport pads** — Some rooms have teleporters for quick travel.

#### Combat
- Some rooms contain **enemies** blocking the path.
- Spend **Sword icons** equal to the enemy's strength to defeat them and gain rewards.

#### Buying Cards
- Spend **Skill** to purchase cards from the market row or reserve piles.
- Purchased cards go to your discard pile (available next shuffle).
- Better cards provide more resources, less Clank, and special abilities.

#### Hacking
- **Command posts** on the board must be hacked to progress through certain areas.
- You must hack at least **2 command posts** before you can enter the escape pod area.
- Hacking requires being on the data port space and spending a hack action.

#### Artifacts
- Artifacts are the primary objective. Each has a VP value.
- You can only carry **1 artifact**.
- Artifacts deeper in the ship are worth more VP but are harder to reach and escape from.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Clank Mechanic
- Cards with Clank icons force you to add your colored cubes to the **Clank area**.
- When a **Boss Attack** triggers, all cubes in the Clank area are dumped into the **dragon bag** along with new black cubes.
- Cubes are drawn from the bag: **your color = 1 damage per cube drawn**. Black cubes are safe.
- If you take **too much damage**, you are knocked out.

#### Boss Attacks (Lord Eradikus)
- Triggered when certain cards appear in the market row.
- The attack intensity **escalates** as players pick up artifacts.
- More cubes are drawn as the game progresses.
- After the first player escapes, Eradikus attacks become automatic each round.

#### Knocked Out
- If you are knocked out **inside the escape pod area**, you still score (rescued by allies).
- If knocked out **deep in the ship**, you score nothing — your artifact and secrets are lost.
- This is the core push-your-luck tension: go deeper for better artifacts but risk not escaping.

#### Escape Pods
- To win, you must grab an artifact, hack at least 2 data ports, and reach an escape pod.
- Once you escape, you are safe from further boss attacks.
- The first player to escape triggers accelerated boss attacks for remaining players.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Triggering the End
- The game ends when **all players** have either escaped or been knocked out.
- After the first escape, remaining players face increasingly dangerous boss attacks.

#### Final Scoring (only if you escaped or were knocked out in the escape area)
- **Artifact** — VP value of your artifact (5-35 VP range).
- **Secret tokens** — Flip and score face-down secrets collected.
- **Credits** — Remaining credits convert to VP.
- **Market cards** — Many purchased cards have VP printed on them.

#### Eliminated Players
- If knocked out deep in the ship (not near escape pods), you score **0 points**.

#### Winner
Highest total VP wins. Tiebreaker: player who escaped first.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Buy efficient cards early** — Cards that give Boots and Skill with no Clank are premium purchases.
- **Thin your deck** — Remove or avoid playing Stumble cards. Trashing weak cards makes your deck more consistent.
- **Plan your route** — Look at artifact placement and plan which data ports to hack on the way.
- **Don't rush too deep** — Build your deck for 3-4 turns before venturing into dangerous territory.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Going too deep without an escape plan** — The deepest artifacts are worth more, but if you can't escape, you score 0.
- **Ignoring Clank management** — Every Clank cube in the bag is a chance to take damage. Minimize unnecessary noise.
- **Not hacking enough** — You must hack 2 data ports minimum. Don't forget this requirement.
- **Buying too many expensive cards** — Expensive cards clog your deck if you don't have enough skill generation.
- **Waiting too long to grab an artifact** — Once someone escapes, attacks accelerate. Have your artifact before that happens.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Which artifact to target** — Higher value = deeper and more dangerous. Balance risk and reward.
- **When to turn back** — Every turn deeper makes escape harder. Know when to pivot.
- **Card purchasing** — Movement cards vs. attack cards vs. VP cards vs. Clank-free cards.
- **Secret token collection** — Worth detouring for secrets? They can be very valuable or mediocre.
- **Timing your escape** — Escaping first triggers pressure on opponents, which is strategically powerful.
